message:
feat: Add bash unit tests for detect_platform and normalize_dropbox_url

- Add 20 bats-core tests for bash functions in giil
- Test detect_platform for all supported platforms (icloud, dropbox, gphotos, gdrive)
- Test subdomain spoofing protection (domain boundary validation)
- Test normalize_dropbox_url for raw=1 parameter handling
- Update run-all.sh to include bash tests when bats is available

Test count: 152 JS + 20 bash = 172 tests total

🤖 Generated with [Claude Code](https://claude.com/claude-code)

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.5 <noreply@anthropic.com>

 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-84w","title":"Task: Unit tests for EXIF datetime extraction (getExifDateTime)","description":"## What
Test getExifDateTime() function that extracts datetime from image EXIF metadata.

## Function Behavior
Returns ISO 8601 datetime string or null if not found.

## Test Cases
1. JPEG with DateTimeOriginal field
2. JPEG with only DateTimeDigitized (fallback)
3. JPEG with only DateTime (fallback)
4. JPEG with no datetime fields
5. PNG (no EXIF support typically)
6. HEIC with datetime
7. Corrupt/truncated buffer
8. Empty buffer

## Test Fixtures Required
- scripts/tests/fixtures/images/sample-jpeg-with-exif.jpg
- Create with known datetime for deterministic test

## File
scripts/tests/exif-datetime.test.mjs

## Note
Uses 'exifr' library internally - tests should use real image buffers

 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-8x5","title":"Task: E2E test for Google Drive platform (experimental)","description":"## What
Create E2E integration test for Google Drive downloads. Marked as [experimental].

## Test Script
scripts/tests/e2e/google-drive.test.sh

## Prerequisites
- Need a stable Google Drive test URL (shared as 'Anyone with link')
- Store expected SHA256 in fixtures

## Test Steps
1. Download from drive.google.com/file/d/.../view URL
2. Verify file downloaded (may be lower resolution)
3. Verify JSON output schema (platform: 'gdrive')
4. Log any auth detection

## Verbose Logging
```
[TIME] [INFO] Platform: gdrive [EXPERIMENTAL]
[TIME] [INFO] URL: https://drive.google.com/file/d/...
[TIME] [INFO] Attempting direct download...
[TIME] [WARN] Direct download failed, trying thumbnail extraction
[TIME] [INFO] Downloaded via thumbnail: 1920x1080 (thumbnail quality)
[TIME] [WARN] Full resolution not available (expected for Drive)
[TIME] [PASS] Downloaded successfully with fallback
```

## Error Scenario Test
Also test with a NON-public file to verify auth detection:
```
[TIME] [INFO] Testing auth detection...
[TIME] [INFO] Using restricted test URL
[TIME] [FAIL] Exit code 11 (AUTH_REQUIRED) - expected
[TIME] [PASS] Auth detection working correctly
```

## Notes
- Google Drive is experimental - quality may vary
- Auth detection is critical to avoid capturing login pages
- Test should pass even if resolution is degraded

 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-9ei","title":"FEATURE: Test Infrastructure - Logging, Fixtures, CI Pipeline","description":"## Overview

Build robust test infrastructure that supports both unit and E2E tests with:
- Verbose structured logging for CI debugging
- Real test fixtures (actual image files, not mocks)
- CI pipeline enhancements
- Local development convenience scripts

## Logging Framework

### Requirements
- JSON-structured logs for CI parsing
- Human-readable format for local dev
- Timing metrics for performance regression detection
- Clear PASS/FAIL status per test

### Implementation
```bash
# scripts/tests/lib/log.sh
log_info() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [INFO] $*" >&2; }
log_pass() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [PASS] $*" >&2; }
log_fail() { echo "[$(date -Iseconds)] [FAIL] $*" >&2; }
log_json() { echo "{\"ts\":\"$(date -Iseconds)\",\"level\":\"$1\",\"msg\":\"$2\"}" >&2; }
```

## Test Fixtures

### scripts/tests/fixtures/
```
fixtures/
├── images/
│   ├── sample-jpeg-with-exif.jpg    # Real JPEG with EXIF datetime
│   ├── sample-png.png               # Basic PNG
│   ├── sample-heic.heic             # HEIC for conversion tests
│   └── sample-webp.webp             # WebP format
├── magic-bytes/
│   ├── jpeg-magic.bin               # First 12 bytes of JPEG
│   ├── html-magic.bin               # HTML document start
│   └── heic-magic.bin               # HEIC ftyp header
└── expected-hashes/
    ├── icloud.sha256
    ├── dropbox.sha256
    └── google-photos.sha256
```

### Source for Fixtures
- Use royalty-free test images
- Generate minimal valid images programmatically where possible
- Store actual EXIF data for datetime extraction tests

## CI Pipeline Enhancements

### .github/workflows/ci.yml additions

```yaml
# Add after existing unit-tests job
e2e-tests:
  name: E2E Integration Tests
  needs: [shellcheck, syntax-check, unit-tests]
  runs-on: ubuntu-latest
  timeout-minutes: 30
  strategy:
    fail-fast: false
    matrix:
      platform: [icloud, dropbox, google-photos]
  steps:
    - uses: actions/checkout@v4
    - name: Setup dependencies
      run: ...
    - name: Run ${{ matrix.platform }} test
      run: bash scripts/tests/e2e/${{ matrix.platform }}.test.sh
      env:
        GIIL_LOG_FORMAT: json
```

## Local Development Scripts

### scripts/tests/run-all.sh
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Run all tests: unit + e2e

echo '=== Unit Tests ==='
./scripts/tests/run-tests.sh

echo '=== E2E Tests ==='
for test in ./scripts/tests/e2e/*.test.sh; do
  echo "--- $(basename $test) ---"
  bash "$test"
done
```

### scripts/tests/run-quick.sh
```bash
# Fast tests only (unit tests, skip live E2E)
node --test scripts/tests/*.test.mjs
```

Commit b52b82c."}
-{"id":"get_icloud_image_link-aqm","title":"Task: E2E tests for album mode (--all)","description":"## What
Create E2E tests for album mode that downloads all photos from a shared album.

## Test Script
scripts/tests/e2e/album-mode.test.sh

## Prerequisites
- Need test albums with known photo counts:
  - iCloud album with 3 photos
  - Google Photos album with 3 photos

## Test Steps
```bash
# Create temp output dir
output_dir=$(mktemp -d)

# Download album
giil 'ALBUM_URL' --all --output "$output_dir" --json > output.json

# Verify correct number of files
count=$(ls "$output_dir"/*.jpg | wc -l)
assert_equal "$count" 3

# Verify each file is valid image
for f in "$output_dir"/*.jpg; do
  file "$f" | grep -q 'JPEG' || fail "Invalid image: $f"
done
```

## Verbose Logging
```
[TIME] [INFO] Album mode enabled
[TIME] [INFO] URL: https://share.icloud.com/photos/...
[TIME] [INFO] Detecting album structure...
[TIME] [INFO] Found 3 photos in album
[TIME] [INFO] Downloading 1/3...
[TIME] [INFO] Downloading 2/3...
[TIME] [INFO] Downloading 3/3...
[TIME] [PASS] All 3 photos downloaded successfully
```

## Edge Cases
- Empty album (should exit gracefully)
- Single-photo 'album' (should work like normal mode)
- Very large album (test with limit if >10 photos)

 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-ci6","title":"Task: E2E tests for output modes (--json, --base64, --preserve, --convert)","description":"## What
Create E2E tests that verify all output mode flags work correctly.

## Test Script
scripts/tests/e2e/output-modes.test.sh

## Output Modes to Test

### --json
```bash
output=$(giil 'URL' --json)
# Verify valid JSON
echo "$output" | jq .
# Verify required fields
echo "$output" | jq -e '.ok and .schema_version and .platform and .path'
```

### --base64
```bash
output=$(giil 'URL' --base64)
# Verify valid base64 (decode should work)
echo "$output" | base64 -d > /tmp/test.jpg
file /tmp/test.jpg | grep -q 'JPEG'
```

### --base64 --json (combined)
```bash
output=$(giil 'URL' --base64 --json)
# Verify JSON with base64 field instead of path
echo "$output" | jq -e '.base64'
```

### --preserve
```bash
# Download with --preserve (skip MozJPEG)
giil 'URL' --preserve --json
# Verify file size matches original (larger than default compressed)
```

### --convert webp
```bash
giil 'URL' --convert webp --json
# Verify output extension is .webp
output=$(giil 'URL' --convert webp --json)
echo "$output" | jq -r '.path' | grep -q '\.webp$'
```

### --quality 50
```bash
# Download with lower quality
giil 'URL' --quality 50 --json
# Verify smaller file size
```

## Verbose Logging
Log file sizes to verify compression is working:
```
[TIME] [INFO] Testing output modes...
[TIME] [INFO] Default (MozJPEG): 456 KB
[TIME] [INFO] --preserve: 1.2 MB
[TIME] [INFO] --quality 50: 234 KB
[TIME] [INFO] --convert webp: 189 KB
[TIME] [PASS] All output modes working
```

 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-emm","title":"Task: Unit tests for Google Photos URL extraction (extractGooglePhotosBaseUrl)","description":"## What
Test extractGooglePhotosBaseUrl() function that transforms CDN URLs for full-resolution access.

## Function Behavior
```javascript
// Input: https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/pw/xxx=w1920-h1080
// Output: { baseUrl: 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/pw/xxx', fullResUrl: 'https://lh3.googleusercontent.com/pw/xxx=s0' }
```

## Test Cases
1. Standard CDN URL with size modifier (=w1920-h1080)
2. CDN URL with =s suffix (=s1920)
3. CDN URL without any modifier
4. Non-Google Photos URL (should return unchanged)
5. Empty string / null input
6. URL with query parameters after modifier

## File
scripts/tests/google-photos.test.mjs

## Notes
- The =s0 modifier requests maximum resolution
- This is the key optimization for Google Photos full-res downloads

 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-es6","title":"Task: E2E test for Dropbox platform","description":"## What
Create E2E integration test for Dropbox downloads using the fast path (no Playwright).

## Test Script
scripts/tests/e2e/dropbox.test.sh

## Prerequisites
- Need a stable Dropbox test URL (user to provide/create)
- Store expected SHA256 in fixtures

## Test Steps
1. Download from Dropbox /s/ format URL
2. Verify no Playwright was used (fast path)
3. Verify output file exists and is valid image
4. Verify JSON output schema (platform: 'dropbox', method: 'direct')
5. Log timing (should be 1-3 seconds, not 5-15)
6. Verify SHA256 matches expected

## Verbose Logging
```
[TIME] [INFO] Platform: dropbox
[TIME] [INFO] Testing fast path (no browser)
[TIME] [INFO] URL: https://www.dropbox.com/s/...
[TIME] [INFO] Download started
[TIME] [INFO] Download complete: 2.4 MB in 1.8s
[TIME] [INFO] Method: direct (expected)
[TIME] [PASS] SHA256 verified
[TIME] [PASS] Total time: 2.1s (under 5s threshold)
```

## Also Test
- /scl/fi/ URL format (alternate sharing format)
- Raw URL with existing dl=0 parameter
- Raw URL with existing raw=1 parameter (should work)

## Exit Codes to Verify
- Exit 0: Success
- Exit 10: Network timeout (if Dropbox is down)
- Exit 11: Auth required (password-protected link)
- Exit 12: Not found (deleted/expired)
","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:42:30.058243036-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-04T03:28:01.902274063-05:00","closed_at":"2026-01-04T03:28:01.902274063-05:00","close_reason":"Completed as part of fmq. scripts/tests/e2e/dropbox.test.sh created with shared common.sh library.","dependencies":[{"issue_id":"get_icloud_image_link-es6","depends_on_id":"get_icloud_image_link-fmq","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:42:30.088888557-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"get_icloud_image_link-es6","depends_on_id":"get_icloud_image_link-9ei","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-04T02:42:30.094396526-05:00","created_by":"ubuntu"}]}

 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-hdm.7.2","title":"Update AGENTS.md architecture diagram","description":"## What

Update the architecture diagram in AGENTS.md to reflect v3 changes, specifically the Dropbox fast path that bypasses extractor.mjs.

## Current Diagram (INCORRECT for v3)

```
        ┌─────────┐          ┌──────────┐          ┌──────────┐
        │ Dropbox │          │  Google  │          │  iCloud/ │
        │ (curl)  │          │  Photos  │          │  GDrive  │
        └─────────┘          └──────────┘          └──────────┘
             │                     │                     │
             ▼                     ▼                     ▼
        ┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
        │              extractor.mjs (Playwright)                  │
```

This shows Dropbox going to extractor.mjs, but v3 bypasses it entirely!

## Corrected Diagram (v3)

```
┌─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┐
│                    URL Input                                     │
└─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────┘
                              │
                              ▼
                    ┌──────────────────┐
                    │ detect_platform()│
                    └──────────────────┘
                              │
        ┌─────────────────────┼─────────────────────┐
        ▼                     ▼                     ▼
   ┌─────────┐          ┌──────────┐          ┌──────────┐
   │ Dropbox │          │  Google  │          │  iCloud/ │
   │ FAST    │          │  Photos  │          │  GDrive  │
   └─────────┘          └──────────┘          └──────────┘
        │                     │                     │
        │                     ▼                     ▼
        │            ┌─────────────────────────────────────────┐
        │            │        extractor.mjs (Playwright)        │
        │            │                                           │
        │            │   TIER 1: Download button (9 selectors)   │
        │            │      ↓ fail                               │
        │            │   TIER 2: Network CDN interception        │
        │            │      ↓ fail                               │
        │            │   TIER 3: Element screenshot              │
        │            │      ↓ fail                               │
        │            │   TIER 4: Viewport screenshot             │
        │            └─────────────────────────────────────────┘
        │                           │
        ▼                           ▼
   ┌─────────┐            ┌──────────────────┐
   │  curl   │            │ Image Processing │
   │ raw=1   │            │  Sharp + MozJPEG │
   └─────────┘            │  EXIF extraction │
        │                 │  HEIC conversion │
        │                 └──────────────────┘
        │                           │
        └───────────────┬───────────┘
                        ▼
              ┌──────────────────┐
              │     Output       │
              │ (file/json/b64)  │
              └──────────────────┘
```

## Key Changes to Highlight

1. **Dropbox exits early** - Direct curl download, no Playwright
2. **Platform-specific paths** - Each platform has tailored strategy
3. **Shared output layer** - All paths converge for final output

## Also Update

1. Exit codes table (add new codes 10-13, 20)
2. CLI options table (add new flags)
3. Supported Platforms table (already present, verify accuracy)

 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-i8w","title":"Task: Create test logging framework (lib/log.sh)","description":"## What
Create a shared logging library for all E2E test scripts with:
- Timestamped log messages
- Color-coded severity levels
- JSON output mode for CI parsing
- Pass/fail counters

## File
scripts/tests/lib/log.sh

## Implementation
```bash
#!/usr/bin/env bash
# Test logging library

# Colors
RED='\033[0;31m'
GREEN='\033[0;32m'
YELLOW='\033[0;33m'
BLUE='\033[0;34m'
NC='\033[0m' # No Color

# Counters
PASS_COUNT=0
FAIL_COUNT=0

timestamp() { date -Iseconds; }

log_info() { echo -e "${BLUE}[$(timestamp)] [INFO]${NC} $*" >&2; }
log_warn() { echo -e "${YELLOW}[$(timestamp)] [WARN]${NC} $*" >&2; }
log_pass() { 
  echo -e "${GREEN}[$(timestamp)] [PASS]${NC} $*" >&2
  ((PASS_COUNT++))
}
log_fail() {
  echo -e "${RED}[$(timestamp)] [FAIL]${NC} $*" >&2
  ((FAIL_COUNT++))
}

# JSON mode (for CI)
log_json() {
  local level=$1 msg=$2
  echo "{\"ts\":\"$(timestamp)\",\"level\":\"$level\",\"msg\":\"$msg\"}"
}

# Summary at end of test
test_summary() {
  echo ""
  echo "=== Test Summary ==="
  echo "Passed: $PASS_COUNT"
  echo "Failed: $FAIL_COUNT"
  if [[ $FAIL_COUNT -gt 0 ]]; then
    exit 1
  fi
}

# Assertions
assert_exit_code() {
  local expected=$1 actual=$2 msg=${3:-"Exit code check"}
  if [[ "$actual" == "$expected" ]]; then
    log_pass "$msg (exit $actual)"
  else
    log_fail "$msg: expected $expected, got $actual"
  fi
}

assert_file_exists() {
  local path=$1
  if [[ -f "$path" ]]; then
    log_pass "File exists: $path"
  else
    log_fail "File missing: $path"
  fi
}
```

## Usage
```bash
source scripts/tests/lib/log.sh

log_info "Starting test..."
log_pass "Download successful"
log_fail "SHA mismatch"
test_summary
```

 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-jrg","title":"Task: Unit tests for filename generation (generateFilename)","description":"## What
Test generateFilename() function that creates output filenames from platform, datetime, and index.

## Function Behavior
```javascript
// Platform + datetime: icloud_20240115_143245.jpg
// Platform + index: icloud_001.jpg  
// Platform only: icloud_<timestamp>.jpg
```

## Test Cases
1. With valid datetime and no index
2. With index and no datetime  
3. With both datetime and index
4. With neither (falls back to current timestamp)
5. Different platforms (icloud, dropbox, gphotos, gdrive)
6. Different extensions (jpg, png, webp)
7. Edge case: datetime with timezone
8. Edge case: very large index

-{"id":"get_icloud_image_link-m4d","title":"Task: Unit tests for bash functions (detect_platform, normalize_url)","description":"## What
Create unit tests for pure bash functions in giil script.

## Approach
Use bats-core (Bash Automated Testing System) for bash function testing.

## Functions to Test

### detect_platform (bash version)
```bash
@test 'detect_platform returns icloud for share.icloud.com' {
  result=$(detect_platform 'https://share.icloud.com/photos/abc123')
  [ "$result" = 'icloud' ]
}

@test 'detect_platform returns dropbox for dropbox.com/s/' {
  result=$(detect_platform 'https://www.dropbox.com/s/abc123/photo.jpg')
  [ "$result" = 'dropbox' ]
}
```

### normalize_url
```bash
@test 'normalize_url converts icloud.com/photos# to share.icloud.com' {
  result=$(normalize_url 'https://www.icloud.com/photos/#abc123')
  [ "$result" = 'https://share.icloud.com/photos/abc123' ]
}
```

### normalize_dropbox_url
```bash
@test 'normalize_dropbox_url adds raw=1' {
  result=$(normalize_dropbox_url 'https://www.dropbox.com/s/abc/photo.jpg?dl=0')
  [[ "$result" == *'raw=1'* ]]
}
```

## Setup
1. Install bats-core: `npm install -g bats`
2. Create scripts/tests/bash/giil.bats
3. Source giil functions for testing

## File
scripts/tests/bash/giil.bats

## Notes
- Bats is widely used for bash testing
- Tests run in isolated subshells
- Supports setup/teardown hooks

 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-qoh","title":"EPIC: Comprehensive Test Coverage for giil v3.0","description":"## Overview

giil currently has minimal test coverage:
- 2 unit test files (platform-detection, content-validation)
- 1 integration test (iCloud only, no Dropbox/Google Photos/Google Drive)
- No error scenario tests
- No output mode tests (--base64, --preserve, --convert)
- No verbose logging in test infrastructure

## Goal

Achieve comprehensive test coverage using REAL tests (no mocks/fakes) with detailed logging for debugging. All tests should:
1. Test actual functionality against real or realistic data
2. Provide verbose, structured logging for CI debugging
3. Cover all code paths and edge cases
4. Support both local development and CI execution

## Major Gaps to Fill

### Unit Tests (Pure Functions)
- URL normalization (normalize_url, normalize_dropbox_url)
- EXIF datetime extraction (getExifDateTime)
- Filename generation (generateFilename)
- JSON output formatting (formatJsonSuccess, formatJsonError)
- Google Photos URL extraction (extractGooglePhotosBaseUrl)
- Exit code error classification
- processAndSaveImage logic branches

### Integration Tests (E2E)
- Multi-platform: iCloud, Dropbox, Google Photos, Google Drive
- Error scenarios: timeout, auth required, not found, unsupported type
- Output modes: --json, --base64, --preserve, --convert
- Album mode: --all for different platforms
- CLI flags: --verbose, --trace, --print-url, --debug-dir

### Bash Layer Tests
- CLI argument parsing
- Platform detection (bash version)
- Dependency detection
- Version consistency

### Infrastructure
- Verbose structured logging (JSON format for CI parsing)
- Test fixtures with real image files
- Replay tests for deterministic results

## Success Criteria
- 90%+ code coverage on testable pure functions
- All 4 platforms have at least 1 live integration test
- All exit codes have corresponding error scenario tests
- All CLI flags have at least 1 test
- Detailed logging makes CI failures debuggable

 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-s1d","title":"Task: Unit tests for error code mapping (ErrorMap)","description":"## What
Test ErrorMap constant that maps error codes to exit codes and messages.

## ErrorMap Structure
```javascript
const ErrorMap = {
  'CAPTURE_FAILURE': ExitCodes.CAPTURE_FAILURE,
  'NETWORK_ERROR': ExitCodes.NETWORK_ERROR,
  'AUTH_REQUIRED': ExitCodes.AUTH_REQUIRED,
  'NOT_FOUND': ExitCodes.NOT_FOUND,
  ...
};
```

## Test Cases
1. All error codes map to correct exit codes
2. Error codes match ExitCodes values
3. All documented exit codes (0-20) have mappings
4. Edge case: unknown error code handling

 {"id":"get_icloud_image_link-tfk","title":"FEATURE: Unit Test Expansion - Pure JavaScript Functions","description":"## Overview

Expand unit test coverage for all pure JavaScript functions embedded in giil. These tests use Node.js native test runner and extract functions via extract-functions.mjs.

## Current Coverage
- ✅ detectPlatform() - platform-detection.test.mjs
- ✅ validateContentType() - content-validation.test.mjs  
- ✅ validateMagicBytes() - content-validation.test.mjs
- ✅ validateImageContent() - content-validation.test.mjs

## Missing Coverage (HIGH PRIORITY)
1. **formatJsonSuccess/formatJsonError** - JSON output schema v3
2. **extractGooglePhotosBaseUrl** - Google Photos =s0 URL extraction
3. **getExifDateTime** - EXIF metadata parsing
4. **generateFilename** - Filename from datetime/index

## Missing Coverage (MEDIUM PRIORITY)
5. **downloadGooglePhotosFullRes** - Full-res download logic
6. **parseGoogleDriveId** - File ID extraction from Drive URLs
7. **getGoogleDriveDownloadUrl/getGoogleDrivePreviewUrl** - URL builders
8. **ErrorMap** - Error code to exit code mapping

## Testing Approach
- Extract functions via extract-functions.mjs (already exists)
- Use real data where possible (actual EXIF buffers, real URLs)
- Each function gets its own describe() block
- Test edge cases: null, undefined, empty, malformed inputs

## Files to Create/Modify
- scripts/tests/json-formatting.test.mjs (NEW)
- scripts/tests/google-photos.test.mjs (NEW)
- scripts/tests/exif-datetime.test.mjs (NEW)
- scripts/tests/filename-generation.test.mjs (NEW)
- scripts/tests/error-mapping.test.mjs (NEW)
- scripts/tests/extract-functions.mjs (MODIFY - add new extractors)

## Dependencies
- Requires extract-functions.mjs updates to export new functions
- Some tests need sample image buffers (JPEG with EXIF, HEIC, etc.)

@@ -0,0 +1,14 @@
+#!/usr/bin/env bash
+# Run bash unit tests using bats
+set -euo pipefail
+
+SCRIPT_DIR="$(cd "$(dirname "${BASH_SOURCE[0]}")" && pwd)"
+
+# Check for bats
+if ! command -v bats &> /dev/null; then
+    echo "[error] bats is not installed. Install with: npm install -g bats" >&2
+    exit 1
+fi
+
+echo "=== Bash Unit Tests ==="
+bats "$SCRIPT_DIR"/*.bats

@@ -14,16 +14,30 @@ total_passed=0
 total_failed=0
 start_time=$SECONDS
 
-# Run unit tests
-log_section "Unit Tests"
+# Run JavaScript unit tests
+log_section "JavaScript Unit Tests"
 if node --test "$SCRIPT_DIR"/*.test.mjs; then
-    log_pass "All unit tests passed"
+    log_pass "All JavaScript unit tests passed"
     ((total_passed+=1))
 else
-    log_fail "Some unit tests failed"
+    log_fail "Some JavaScript unit tests failed"
     ((total_failed+=1))
 fi
 
+# Run Bash unit tests if bats is available
+if command -v bats &> /dev/null && compgen -G "$SCRIPT_DIR/bash/*.bats" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
+    log_section "Bash Unit Tests"
+    if bats "$SCRIPT_DIR"/bash/*.bats; then
+        log_pass "All Bash unit tests passed"
+        ((total_passed+=1))
+    else
+        log_fail "Some Bash unit tests failed"
+        ((total_failed+=1))
+    fi
+else
+    log_info "Skipping Bash tests (bats not installed or no tests found)"
+fi
+
 # Run E2E tests if they exist
 if compgen -G "$SCRIPT_DIR/e2e/*.test.sh" > /dev/null 2>&1; then
     log_section "E2E Tests"
